entry.content: Removed

entry.title: Manage Risk-free Rate (RFR) Interest Rate Instrument with Accumulating Condition
entry.content:
Foreword

With the progress of IBOR (Inter-bank offered rate) transition, more and more financial instruments (such as OTC interesting bearing instruments, bonds, interest rate swaps, etc.) are adopting alternative nearly risk-free rates (RFRs), like SOFR for USD, €STR(ESTER) for EUR, SONIA for GBP, SARON for CHF, etc.

This article outlines the process of creating a typical SOFR-based Interest Rate Instrument transaction using product type 55A which is pre-delivered in best practices configuration content of SAP in S/4HANA Public Cloud, Treasury and Risk Management Solution, and this transaction utilizes the accumulating condition function which is available since 2308 release.

Step-by-Step Guide

1. Create the 55A investment transaction.

On the Structure tab, enter the necessary parameters.

Go to the details of the Normal Interest condition and change parameters:

Go to the Dates tab of Nominal Interest condition, basically the ‘dates’ information here reflects that interests are paid weekly, and the first due and payment date is 02.05.2024.

Go to the Interest Rate Adjustment condition, and change the parameters.

(Note: all of the above maintained data in the transaction should be based on the contract details specified by counterparty.)

Go to Conditions Overview, select the Nominal Interest condition, and then choose Create Accumulating Condition.

On the dialog box, choose condition type 1205 (which is from SAP Best Practices Configuration Content for Public Cloud since 2308 release), and choose OK.

Confirm the Eff. From date (= eff. from date of nominal interest condition) by choosing Copy.

On the next screen, you will see the details for the new accumulating condition within a new condition group 7500. You may check the ‘dates’ information of this accumulating condition, which should be carried from and equal to the ‘dates’ information of the Nominal Interest condition.

Go to the Cash Flow tab, by default the accumulating interest flows (1205) are displayed.

By clicking button Show/Hide Single Flows, the single interest flows (1200) will also show up.

With this kind of setup in the transaction, we have achieved the objectives:

  • The single interest flows (1200) are calculated based on daily SOFR rates and compounded daily (except weekend on which the Friday rate is used, and Friday ~ Sunday are calculated and compounded together), within one interest period.
  • The last single interest flow of an interest period uses the SOFR rate of previous day (because of lockout period = 1 working day).
  • An accumulating interest flow (1205) sums up the single interest flows of this interest period.
  • Only accumulating interest flow will be relevant to posting and payment, which will be explained later.

Here, from the screen-shot, you see all single flows are using percentage rate 5.322 (=5.31+spead 0.012), that’s because so far the daily SOFR rates imported into system are up to 24.04.2024, therefore SOFR rate 5.31 on 04.24.2024 is used for calculation of future single interest cash flows in this transaction for the time being.

Save the transaction.

2. Settle the transaction (screen-shot is not provided here).

3. Run TBB1 for the initial principal increase flow on 25.04.2024 (screen-shot is not provided here).

4. Now, assume the month end (30.04.2024) is reached.

The SOFR rates until 30.04.2024 have been imported into system.

After SOFR rates are imported, you can update the cash flows via scheduling job for TMFM or changing the transaction (screen-shots are not provided here)

Check the cash flows after being updated:

The last single interest flow of the 1st interest period (cal. from 01.05.2024 to 02.05.2024) uses the SOFR rate on 30.04.2024, because the lockout period = 1 working day specified in Interest Rate Adjustment condition in this example.

5. Although the interest rate fixings have not been conducted for the single interest flows up to 30.04.2024 (via TJ05/TI10), we can run TPM44 to accrue the interest income up to 30.04.2024 based on the forecasted cash flows.

Run TPM44 on key date 30.04.2024 (and key date is included) for one valuation area:

The 4 single interest flows up to 30.04.2024 are selected (although they are displayed by using the accumulating interest update type MM1205+ in the log), and accumulated amount (889.21 USD) is used for accrual posting (instead of accrual posting per each single interest flow).

Before running TPM44, the configuration in IMG activity Assign Update Types for Accrual/Deferral (configuration activity ID 102925 in public cloud) should be completed, for the accumulating interest flow’s update date type (MM1205+), although it is accumulated on the basis of single interest flows (MM1200+), and a Grouping Term should be specified here.

6. Now, the time has come to 02.05.2024, on which the interest payment for the 1st interest period should happen.

And at this point in time, the SOFR date of 01.05.2024 has been known and entered in system.

Run TJ05 to fix interest rates up to 01.05.2024:

Afterwards, check the cash flows in deal:

In this example, the last single interest flow of the 1st interest period (cal. from 01.05.2024 to 02.05.2024) still uses the SOFR rate on 30.04.2024, even if rate of 01.05.2024 is already imported, because the lockout period = 1 working day specified in Interest Rate Adjustment condition.

Nevertheless, the newly imported SOFR rate of 01.05.2024 is used to update the forecasted single interest flows of next interest period. 

Now, the relevant single interest flows in the 1st interest period has the Interest Rate Adjustment Status ‘Fixed’, and relevant single interest flows and accumulating interest flow have posting information ‘Flagged for Fixing’, which means TBB1 can be executed to fix and post the interest payment for the 1st interest period.

7. Run TBB1 up to and including due date 02.05.2024 (display one valuation area as example): posting is only carried out for accumulating interest update type MM1205+. Posting is NOT carried out for single interest update type MM1200+, that’s because when a single interest condition is accumulated by accumulating condition, the relevant single interest flows will become irrelevant to posting, although the posting relevant configuration is complete for single interest update type.

If payment request should be generated for interest flow, then it is also only relevant to the accumulating interest flow.

After running TBB1, check cash flows in transaction again: now single interest flows have posting information ‘Fixed (not relevant for posting)’, and accumulating interest flow has posting information ‘Posting carried out’.

If checking in TPM13, you can’t see the single interest flows, because they are not transferred from transaction management to position management.

Appendix:

More information regarding IBOR transition could be found from SAP notes:

  • 2939657 - Composite SAP Note: EU Benchmark Regulation, Risk-Free Rates (RFRs) - shared basis for CML and TRM
  • 2932789 - Composite SAP Note: EU Benchmark Regulation, Risk-Free Rates (RFRs), TRM
  • 3328022 - Accumulating Interest Flows: Collective Note

entry.title: Now Presenting: Download and Upload of Pages between Systems
entry.content:
Autors: Sylvia Strack, Kattia Jordan-Philipp

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the transport of spaces and pages from a development system to a test or production system (3 system landscape), or from a quality system to a production system (2 system landscape) is done via Software Collection (see System Landscape and Transport Management | SAP Help Portal).

However, so far there was no option to transfer spaces and pages created in a starter system to a development or quality system. You had to manually re-create the spaces and pages in the other (target) system which was time- and effort-consuming.

We can share good news, since this situation changes now.

To create a space is a straightforward activity. However, to create a page demands more time and effort.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408, SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain 2408, and SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Release 2408, SAP releases the download and upload functionality for pages. The functionality focuses on pages only and not on spaces.  

Download and upload of pages is an easy task. It requires the following steps:

Source System:

  1. Logon to the source system (e.g., the Starter system) where you have the page(s) you want to take over to the target system.
  2. Open the Manage Launchpad Pages app.
  3. Select one or multiple pages that you want to take over into another system, e.g., into the Development system.
  4. Press Download on the upper right above the list of pages.

  5. A file of type JSON with the information of the selected pages is created on your computer.


    Target System:

    1. Logon to the target system (e.g., the Development system into which you want to take over the pages you created in the Starter system).
    2. Open the Manage Launchpad Pages app.
    3. Press Upload on the upper right above the list of pages.



    4. Click on Browse and select the previously created JSON file from your computer and click Next.


      Please note, if you are on the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, then you need to enter a transport, that you have created upfront, into the Transport field:



    5. Before the actual upload takes place, a dialog appears with two tabs: the tab Pages That Can Be Uploaded and the tab Pages That Can’t Be Uploaded. For each page that cannot be uploaded, you get the information explaining the reason. A possible reason could for example be that you try to upload a page, that already exists on the target system, by having the same page ID.
      In the tab Pages That Can Be Uploaded, you need to select all pages that you intend to upload to the system. Press Upload and the pages from the JSON file will be uploaded.



    6. The desired pages are imported and shown in the pages list. Continue working with the pages as required. This includes for example, to assign the uploaded pages to spaces in your system.


    We hope the download and upload functionality helps you optimize your pages creation and supports you to achieve greater efficiency and reuse.

    Documentation

    SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Uploading and Downloading Pages | SAP Help Portal

    SAP Integrated Business Planning: Uploading and Downloading Pages | SAP Help Portal

    SAP BTP ABAP Environment Uploading and Downloading Pages | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Target Group

Anyone interested to know more about extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud using SAP Build

 

Background

What is Extensibility

When a customer has unique business requirements that are not fully catered by standard SAP S/4HANA then there is a need to extend the system. These extensions can be classified into 2 broad categories:

  • On-stack extensibility involves extending SAP S/4HANA directly within the core system using key user tools or ABAP.
  • Side-by-side extensibility uses SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to develop custom applications or integrations outside the core S/4HANA system, ensuring greater flexibility and upgrade safety.

What is SAP Build

SAP Build ( including SAP Build Code) is the default extension development toolset on SAP BTP.

Build Tools under low-code spectrum:

  • SAP Build Apps - Create Mobile and Web applications
  • SAP Build Process Automation - Automate business processes and workflows
  • SAP Build Work Zone - Create Digital Workspaces and business sites

Build Tools under Pro-code spectrum:

SAP Build Code - Use Business Application Studio(BAS) and Cloud Application programming Model(CAP) to create Applications. And lot more.

What challenges are we trying to address here

  • Not easy to identify the available extension options for a given S/4HANA application directly in the system
  • Not easy to determine the right extension points (eg APIs, Events) for a given S/4HANA application
  • Not an integrated experience when extending SAP S/4HANA in a side-by-side manner on BTP

 

Introduction

  • A new Extensibility wizard is available with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 that provides both on-stack and side-by-side extension options consolidated into a single wizard.
  • It provides a guided experience to kick start extension creation and enables seamless navigation from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Build.
  • The wizard understands which S/4HANA application it has been started from and accordingly filters the extensions points( like API, Events) based on the business context.

This wizard can be accessed from any extensible SAP S/4HANA app as shown below. "Create Extension" option is available under the Me Area (User menu) on the top right.

On clicking the Create Extension option, the Extensibility Wizard opens up displaying the various extension options

Note: Setup steps mentioned next are needed as a prerequisite to be able to see the "Create Extension" option

 

Setup

Short summary of the setup steps:

  1. Subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation in your SAP BTP subaccount.
  2. Add your SAP S/4HANA cloud system in the BTP Global account under System landscape and register in a formation.
  3. Create 2 destinations in the BTP account pointing to UI service and API service of SAP S/4HANA. This is needed for communication from Build to SAP S/4HANA, for example to fetch business context, events and authorizations.
  4. Create a communication arrangement in SAP S/4HANA. This is to enable communication from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Build.
  5. Assign the required authorization in SAP S/4HANA to access the wizard.

Detailed setup steps:

  • BTP setup steps - link
  • S4 setup steps - link

 

Extension options in the Wizard ( Sep 2024 )

On-stack

Custom Field

Enables addition of new fields in desired business objects. The wizard opens the Custom Field App with the business contexts pre set.

 

Custom Logic

Enables addition of custom logic via Cloud BAdIs. Here as well the wizard opens the Custom Logic App with the business contexts pre set.

 

Side-by-Side

Custom Process

Create a Process in SAP Build Process Automation that starts based on an event from SAP S/4HANA. The business context of the starting SAP S/4HANA app is pre set in the wizard to easily identify the relevant events.

A detailed explanation of this process extension option is available in this blog from my colleague.

The extensibility wizard is expected to grow with more extension options in future.

 

Conclusion

The new SAP Build Extensibility wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides a simple guided experience to create extensions both on-stack and side-by-side.

If you have access to an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system, follow the setup steps and do explore the Extensibility Wizard !

entry.title: SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.4 - What's new?
entry.content:
SAP Warehouse Operator Version 2.4 is now available on the App Store. Let's have a look at the new features. 

With SAP Warehouse Operator 2.4, we are introducing these new key capabilities: 

  • Ad-Hoc warehouse task creation for Handling Units
  • New Targeted Scan to better select a specific barcode among many barcodes
  • Support for low stock checks when confirming warehouse tasks
  • Basic validation profiles to configure required validation fields based on warehouse number and process type
  • Picking of partial quantities from Handling Units

Ad-Hoc warehouse task creation for Handling Units

The creation of Ad-Hoc warehouse tasks for Handling Units is now supported. If you want to move a Handling Unit inside a warehouse, but no warehouse task has been created yet, you may now use the app to create the warehouse task directly on your iPhone. Simply by scanning the Handling Unit and defining the warehouse process type for the task.

You can then walk or drive to the destination bin in the warehouse and scan it with the mobile app to finalize the creation of the task. Of course, you may also directly drop-off the Handling Unit and confirm the task.

 

Targeted Scan

There are occasions in a warehouse, when you want to select a specific barcode among many barcodes. For example, when changing the destination bin or the resource. To better guide the user for these cases, we are introducing a Targeted Scan. A finder on the screen supports the user to better aim at specific barcodes. By tapping on a highlighted barcode on the screen, it is then scanned and selected.

 

Low Stock Checks

The low stock check is a physical inventory procedure that you perform when confirming warehouse taks. Here you assume that a storage bin should only contain a small quantity of a product after a stock removal in accordance with the storage bin data in EWM. You define this quantity in the form of a limit value that is based on the operative unit of measure for the product per storage type. 

The system automatically activates the low stock check as soon as you create a warehouse task for a storage bin that should lie below the defined limit value after the stock removal. After conforming the warehouse task in SAP Warehouse Operator, the mobile app asks you to enter the remaining stock quantity in the storage bin.

 

Validation Profiles

Sometimes, it’s not needed to scan and validate all data when working on a warehouse task. Maybe the product IDs have not been printed out as barcodes in your warehouse. With the help of simple configuration profiles, it’s now possible to define which data doesn’t have to be scanned and validated using the mobile app.   

An administrator may configure the validation file and store it in Mobile Services. The file then gets fetched during the onboarding of the app and the rules are applied to the handling of the warehouse tasks. The validation profiles allow you to skip validation for product IDs and batches specific to a process type and warehouse.  

 

Additional Features

We have also enabled picking from Handling Units. However, only the picking of partial quantities is supported. Full palette picking is not supported.  

SAP Warehouse Operator now also supports Branding and Theming. So, the app can be made available to employees of your company following a consistent user experience.

Do you want to give us feedback inside the mobile app? Please tap on the new Feedback button in the user profile, which will lead you to survey to provide feedback.

 

Try the app and engage with us

Please download the new version of the app and try it out yourself.

 

 

Please leave a comment below if you have questions or feedback and we’ll get back to you.

You may also contact us at SAP_Warehouse_Operator@sap.com if you have questions on the configuration to use the app, the setup process or have specific requirements.

We also encourage you to visit our SAP Mobile Applications community page and click “follow”. We’ll be publishing more informative blog posts. Please post questions in the SAP Extended Warehouse Management area. Want to be notified? Check your profile settings to ensure you have your settings activated.

entry.title: Creating side-by-side process extensions from SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
The release of SAP Build's new extensibility wizard forSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release marks a significant step in streamlining the creation of on-stack extensions (within SAP S/4HANA Cloud) and side-by-side extensions (in SAP BTP) to help keep the core clean. The wizard, which can be accessed directly from any business application that is enabled for key user adaptation, allows key users and even developers to easily create custom process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) that can seamlessly integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. In this blog, we will explore the use cases for process extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how the new wizard is a game-changer for customers looking to enhance their business processes

Side-by-side extensions

Side-by-side extensions run outside of SAP S/4HANA Cloud (or other business application) but interact with the business applications using standard extension points like APIs, events, etc. Side-by-side extensions can technically run on any application platform, nevertheless SAP provides and recommends its dedicated platform for extension: SAP BTP.

What are process extensions

Many applications in SAP S/4HANA use SAP Business Workflow enabling preconfigured workflow scenarios to be reused in various situations. The scenarios can either be implemented without any changes or configured for your business processes by making minor adjustments. On top of that, Flexible workflows allow you to define condition-based workflows for the pre-configured approval processes. For example, the flexible workflow for purchase requisitions allows you to define one-step or multi-step approval processes for purchase requisitions according to your requirements. These are pre-delivered as part of the SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. However, your use case may go beyond a typical pre-delivered approval workflow which needs a side-by-side process extension. Here are some use case patterns which warrants a process extension in SAP BTP:

  • Processes that span across systems.
  • Processes that reach the users who are outside SAP S/4HANA.
  • Use certain capabilities from SAP BTP, like SAP AI Services.
  • Any other requirements that are not covered in the standard processes and workflows.

Example use case

Consider a specific use case where you want to make sure that every invoice created in the system has a reference to a purchase order without user intervention. There are multiple ways this can be achieved including a process extension. A side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (in SAP BTP) can check if a purchase order is assigned for every new supplier invoice created in the system. If not, then the process shall identify the right purchase order using SAP AI Services in SAP BTP like Document Information Extraction, Data Attribute Recommendation, Invoice Object Recommendation, SAP AI Core, etc. When the machine learning model could not find the right match, then it could inform the procurement department member for further action. This process extension would run alongside the standard procurement process, adding the necessary functionality without altering the original process.

Why SAP Build Process Automation?

SAP Build Process Automation in SAP BTP with its rich development tools, integration capability and pre-built content is the recommended process extension platform for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. SAP Build Process Automation has 500+ pre-built content packages for extending SAP and non-SAP applications. Most of those content is free-to-use and can be accessed from SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition 2408 release, the extensibility wizard streamlines the creation of process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation. The process created in SAP Build Process Automation is deployed & executable in SAP BTP and can be triggered automatically when a business event is observed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

Creating a process extension

Let us see how easy it is to create a process extension with the new wizard.

Pre-requisite:

  • Setup SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh

A business event-based triggering of process is a capability of SAP Build Process Automation. It requires SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh to observe the events in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to initiate a process in SAP Build Process Automation. Refer to this blog for the detailed setup instruction.

  • You have the right business role and can access the extensibility wizard as described in previous blog of this series.

Procedure

  • From any application which is enabled for key user adaptation, open the 'Me Area' to choose the new 'Create Extension' option to launch the extensibility wizard.

Choose Process Extension. (If you do not have entitlement to SAP Build Process Automation in the connected SAP BTP tenant, then this option will be disabled with a 'Not subscribed' ribbon in the tile).

Based on the application from where the extension was triggered, the wizard automatically identifies the available extension points (in 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition, only business events are supported. More extension points is planned as future increments).

Choose Business Events, and proceed to next step.

The wizard also recognises the event objects that are relevant for the application and automatically applies a filter on the business object. Of course, you can override these filters and search any event that is needed for your use case. Select the appropriate event object and proceed to the next step.

All events of the selected event object are shown. Select the appropriate event and proceed to the next step.

Give a name and description for your extension project that will be created in SAP Build (SAP BTP). 

Confirm the changes in the Summary step or go back to edit any steps. This creates an extension project for based in the inputs given in the previous steps.

Once the project is created successfully, you can directly navigate to the project in SAP Build. 

When navigated into SAP Build (in SAP BTP), you can see a process automation project with a process created for you. 

When you open the process, you can see that the event selected in the wizard is automatically added. All the context from the selected business event is now available at your disposal. 

You can proceed to use the low-code process builder to create an extension process as needed. You can call any APIs like the SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP Cloud Integration models or any SAP BTP services, you can add a user task that creates a task in My Inbox or SAP Task Center, you can call an RPA bot automation, you can send notification emails, etc. Once the process is created, release a version and deploy the content into SAP BTP. 

When this process automation project is deployed in SAP BTP, it listens to the specific event in the connected SAP S/4HANA Cloud. When such an event is observed, it starts an instance of the process with the context that is available in the business event.

For more information on the lifecycle, refer to the help documentation of SAP Build Process Automation.

Key take aways

  • A brand-new wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is available with 2408 release to create on-stack and side-by-side extensions.
  • The wizard can be called from any application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition that is enabled for key user adaptation.
  • Discover the available process extension points based on the application that was used to start the wizard, through the concept of Business Context.
  • Create a side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Look-ahead plans

  • Management of side-by-side extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • More extension points for process extensions apart from the business event.
  • Enable the wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

entry.title: Responsibility Management: Creating custom teams & responsibility definitions in S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
Hi All,

As we move towards a clean core and an intelligent enterprise, we need a standard framework which can alert the user about the tasks which need their attention. With Responsibility management in S/4 HANA, SAP provides a standard set of apps which will be used to configure teams, assign members and create agent determination rules for standard as well as custom workflows.

OVERVIEW

Common Approaches:

In any S/4 HANA project, there are multiple scenarios where customers need to determine agents for approval of purchase order, invoices, sales order, billing document, Master data creation/change etc. For each of these processes we use different ways to determine agents, like BADI, BRF+ and custom tables.

Challenges:

All of these methods of determining agents depend on IT teams for configuration and coding, as well as not very user friendly for the end user to setup agent determination rules. The current approaches are not very scalable, especially with side-by-side scenarios running SAP BTP, ABAP environment 

In comes Responsibility management.... With this SAP provides a standard framework to create teams and assign members for various scenarios running either on stack or side by side on SAP BTP.

For more details, please refer to the standard documentation here

 

MAIN CONTENT

In the scenario which we will cover today, the workflow runs in SAP Build Process Automation and the teams are created in S/4 HANA and exposed via ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model API.

But before that a quick overview of the terms to be used in the blog

Team category: It is the classification of team by business processes like Finance, supply chain etc.

Team Type: Each team category can have multiple team types, it's breakdown of team by business process like, Finance team for USA and Finance team for UK.

Responsibility Attributes: The attributes based on which teams will be created and members will be assigned to the team.

There are standard team types, team category & responsibility definitions which SAP provides to cater to different scenarios, however in my case I have created custom responsibility definitions, team category and team types.

1. Create a custom team category using Manage team categories app. The team category name will be prefixed by default by identifier which is configured in ABAP transport organizer (ATO). For my case it was ZZ1.

2. Create a team type and assign it to the custom team category.

3. Create custom responsibility definition. To create custom responsibility definition, you have to create CDS views and assign them to the team category.

2. This CDS view has 2 backend tables, one for code and other for text. On top of these 2 tables, 2 CDS views are created.

4.  Function and Function profile needs to be created, this is required to assign the team members to a particular team. The functions can also be used for controlling authorizations, however in this case we are only using functions as a pre-requisite step to create members of a team.

5. Create the team using Manage team and responsibilities app. Once you select a particular team type automatically all the responsibilities assigned to the team category will automatically show up and you can provide the necessary values.

Each of the team member will be business partner. For each SU01 user a corresponding BP needs to be created, then only it will show up in this app.

6. These teams are stored in backend tables but for easy access there are standard data definitions. These can be used in your ABAP code.

7. To expose these teams to external applications which are running side-by-side your S/4 HANA core, standard API's are available. Detailed documentation can be found here.

8. In our case we have created a RAP API using custom entity, to extract the team's data along with some additional parameters and send it to SBPA via a pull mechanism.

PS: The details of RAP API are not part of this blog.

CONCLUSION:

Responsibility management provides a one stop solution for all your agent determination needs. It can help consolidate process and application specific agent determination requirements into a single application. It would help in removing data silos for agent determination rules, provides a scalable framework which can be used across SAP applications with proper authorizations.

Please free to share your thoughts and reach out to me if you have any questions.

SAP S/4HANA SAP BTP, ABAP environment SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition  ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: 主页-洞察
entry.content:
业务需求

CLOUD在2408版本更新后使用了新版本SPACE界面，有客户发现一些用户的主页界面洞察视图里多出了一些磁贴和卡片，客户希望为用户统一关闭洞察视图。

系统演示

主页-洞察

登录管理员用户，并且为管理员权限添加角色：SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC

回到主页，点击个人资料图标，选择调整用户界面

在洞察视图旁边右键，选择个性化我的主页

对洞察磁铁和卡片进行隐藏，选择保存

选择保存UI和激活新版本

注意：尽管管理员可以统一更改主页界面，但用户始终可以通过“我的主页设置”做个性化设置

感谢观看

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA for group reporting: 15+ Customer-Inspired Improvements Delivered in Q3-2024
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA for group reporting: 15+ Customer-Inspired Improvements Delivered in Q3-2024

At SAP, we believe co-innovating with customers is key to developing SAP S/4HANA for group reporting. Using our Customer Influence Platform (CIP), you have the chance to share and vote for ideas to improve our software. We just finished our Q3-2024 CIP review. We're proud to announce that we delivered over 15 of your ideas this quarter:

  • Six improvements for data collection, including a new dashboard view of Group Reporting Data Collection jobs by status
  • Seven improvements of the consolidation process, including the ability to adjust the partner unit during balance carry forward
  • Three improvements for analytics, including the possibility to combine data and use reporting rules in SAP Analytics Cloud

--
Data Collection

Dashboard view of Group Reporting Data Collection jobs by status
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/302995
We've released a new dashboard view of Group Reporting Data Collection jobs by status. The dashboard includes a consolidated view of jobs that have ended in error for a given period. This new feature significantly improves error monitoring. Also, the dashboard makes it much easier to operate Group Reporting Data Collection with many scheduled jobs. The feature is available since release 2408.

Upload of txt files via Group Reporting Data Collection
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/325741
You can now upload "txt" files via Group Reporting Data Collection. Previously, only csv files could be selected and uploaded using the Group Reporting mapping functionality. With this new feature, you can now upload txt files directly, eliminating the need to convert them to csv format. This improvement deals with the issue where many source systems, such as SAP ECC (ERP Central Component), generate txt files as standard export files. By allowing txt file uploads, the process becomes less time-consuming and reduces the likelihood of errors. The feature is available since release 2408.

Automated load of trial balances from non-SAP sources
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/323113
The feature is native in the data mapping app of Group Reporting Data Collection. With this app you can extract data from any system using custom connections with OData services, transform the data and load it to group reporting. Additionally you can schedule the trial balance import, to further automate the data load process. 

Ability to enter multiple units of measure on a single financial statement item
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/309130
We've implemented the ability to enter/upload multiple units of measure for a single financial statement item into group reporting, for example through Group Reporting Data Collection. This enhancement addresses the limitation where group reporting only accepted a single unit of measure per financial statement item. With this improvement, group reporting fully supports multiple units of measures for a single item, enabling support for more non-financial scenarios such as collecting sales quantities and emitted greenhouse gas on the same financial statement item, such as revenues. Now, for example, Group Reporting Data Collection uploads will allow for the loading of multiple units of measure. The feature is available since release 2408.

Form design - Define links between forms to facilitate navigation
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/260500
You can now define links between forms to facilitate navigation in Group Reporting Data Collection. Previously, navigation within forms wasn't seamless, causing potential inefficiencies during data entry. With this new feature, you can now define links between forms and even create a home form that serves as a central navigation hub. This enhancement eases navigation within forms, making the process of data entry more intuitive and efficient. The feature is available since release 2408.

Document type used for realignment posting should be separated from normal FI postings
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/306306
There's now a dedicated document type 'GR' for realignment postings, separate from normal FI postings. This makes it easier for users to identify and manage the technical realignment documents, maintaining better control and governance over the posting process. Additionally, it ensures that sequential numbering and filing requirements for FI documents are not affected. Overall, this improvement streamlines the process and enhances the overall efficiency of the system.

--
Consolidation process

Historical amounts (equity & investment in subsidiaries)
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/323112
We've improved the historical amounts management in group reporting, addressing the need for a simplified historical amounts process for companies that require the use of a spot rate for the translation of equity and investments in subsidiaries. This allows easy upload of historical amounts in the reporting currency against specific account codes and provides an alternative for the upload of historical rates if preferred. These amounts or rates are then used in the translation of these account codes from the functional currency to the reporting currency. This improvement is beneficial as it ensures accurate and GAAP-compliant translation of historical amounts in equity and investments in subsidiaries, which is fundamental to the consolidation process. Additionally, it simplifies the historical amounts process and eliminates the need for clunky workarounds, providing greater efficiency and accuracy in financial reporting.

Usage of historical foreign exchange rates
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/321619
We've added functionality that now automates the handling of historical foreign exchange rates in group reporting. This change removes the need for the previous manual approach. With this new functionality, the process of using historical foreign exchange rates is automated, reducing the need for manual workarounds. This improvement makes your financial reporting processes quicker and more accurate. It simplifies the steps involved, helping you avoid the risks of manual errors.

Use of separate financial statement items for foreign currency translation
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/321623
You can already define a specific currency translation difference financial statement item for investments and equity financial statement items, this feature is native. It allows for the use of separate financial statement items for foreign currency translation, addressing the need for a more diversified presentation and smoother analysis of translation effects. Additionally, this native feature offers the benefit of automated foreign currency translation without the need for manual adjustments.

Net income postings - Additional characteristics not inherited
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/303814
We've improved the automatic net income posting process so that now additional characteristics such as Partner Segment and Partner Profit Center are automatically inherited. The feature is available since release 2408.

Ability to capture multiple units of measure per breakdown category
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/317703
You can now capture multiple units of measure per breakdown category in group reporting. Previously, you could assign only a single fixed unit of measure per breakdown category. With the new enhancement, business users can enter different units of measure, whether amounts or quantities, for the same financial statement items or accounts. This provides greater flexibility and meets a broader range of business needs. By allowing multiple units of measure per breakdown category, our reporting now accurately reflects the diverse ways businesses measure and track data. This enhancement also reduces the number of financial statement items needed, simplifying and streamlining your reporting structure. The feature is available since release 2408.

Ability to exclude document types from required consolidation tasks
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/324232
We've delivered the ability to exclude document types from required consolidation tasks. This improvement allows for the configuration and maintenance of a selection of document types to be excluded from specific data processing tasks. This enhancement addresses the need to exclude certain document types from tasks such as Elimination, Consolidation of Investment, or Custom Reclass, which may involve postings meant for reporting or presentation purposes and not intended for inclusion in financial statements. With this new feature, there is now an easier way to maintain exclusion/required document types for a given consolidation task, providing more flexibility and efficiency in data processing. The feature is available since release 2108.

Allow partner unit change during balance carry forward
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/312676
You can now adjust the partner unit during the balance carry forward process. This enhancement allows you to modify and adjust the partner unit, providing greater flexibility and control over your data. Additionally, you now have the ability to remove partner information on accounts with optional breakdowns, such as related parties. This ensures cleaner and more precise data management, enhancing the overall consistency of your partner fields. The feature is available since release 2408.

--
Analytics

Group Data Analysis reports should display Profit Center with Non-Compound Key by default
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/325369
The Group Data Analysis (GDA) report can now display Profit Center with Non-Compound Key by default, saving users a significant amount of effort in changing from compound to non-compound values. To enable this display, you can use "Key Not Compound" of Group Financial Statement - Review Booklet. This makes the report more user-friendly for viewing and analysis, aligning with the expected benefit of saving effort and enhancing user experience.

Usage of reporting rules outside of S/4HANA
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/295397
With the API called "API_CNSLDTNRLBSDGRPJRNLITM", you can now use the reporting rules defined in group reporting outside of S/4HANA, providing the ability to report profit and loss, balance sheet, cash flow statement outside of S/4 and enhance reporting capabilities beyond Fiori apps. Additionally, it offers the opportunity to combine data and utilize reporting rules in SAP Analytics Cloud, including on acquired data, with the added benefit of automatically replicating the reporting rules to SAP Analytics Cloud through an interface. The feature available with release 2408.1.

Hierarchy of the consolidation segment not released for consumption in SAP Analytics Cloud
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/269511
We've improved the Core Data Service (CDS) view "I_CnsldtnSegmentHierNode", which provides access to the hierarchy of the consolidation segment, to enable its consumption in SAP Analytics Cloud for reporting purposes. The feature available with release 2402.

--
More

Join SAP Customer Influence: directly influence the development of SAP S/4HANA for group reporting by submitting improvement requests and voting on them, and be notified on the progress of your ideas.

entry.title: Unlocking Efficiency: Choosing the Ideal SAP Warehousing Solution for Your Company
entry.content:
In today's dynamic supply chain landscape, businesses require efficient and flexible warehouse management systems (WMS) to stay competitive. SAP offers robust solutions for optimizing warehouse operations. However, with multiple options available, such as Basic EWM, Advanced EWM, Decentralized EWM, and newer alternatives like SAP Stock Room Management and SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud, choosing the right one can be challenging.

This blog will explore each of these options and provide guidance on selecting the most suitable solution for your business.

Understanding the Different SAP Warehouse Management Options

1. SAP Stock Room Management

  • What It Is: Stock Room Management is a simplified warehouse management solution introduced within SAP S/4HANA. It offers core warehouse functionalities tailored for businesses with straightforward warehouse needs. SAP created Stock Room Management for customers migrating from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management (WM) system to S/4HANA, but who do not require the advanced features of SAP EWM.
  • Key Features:
    • Core warehouse processes like stock transfers, goods receipt, and goods issue.
    • Basic inventory management functionalities.
    • Material handling, cycle counting, and internal movements.
    • Simplified user experience with integration into SAP S/4HANA.
  • Best Suited For: Businesses that require basic warehouse functionalities without the need for advanced automation or complex workflows. It is ideal for smaller warehouses or companies transitioning from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management system who want to stay within the SAP S/4HANA ecosystem without upgrading to full EWM.

2. SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud

  • What It Is: SAP Warehouse Management (WM) on S/4HANA Public Cloud provides cloud-based warehouse management functionalities for businesses that want to move their operations to the cloud. It offers core WM features while leveraging the scalability and flexibility of the public cloud infrastructure.
  • Key Features:
    • Cloud-based deployment with access to core warehouse functionalities.
    • Seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
    • Real-time inventory visibility and warehouse operations.
    • Regular updates from SAP without the need for heavy customizations.
  • Best Suited For: Businesses looking for a scalable, cloud-based solution to manage basic warehouse operations, especially those that want to reduce infrastructure costs and simplify system management. It is an excellent option for companies transitioning to the cloud or that want a more agile and scalable warehouse management solution.

3. Basic EWM (Embedded within SAP S/4HANA)

  • What It Is: Basic EWM is the foundational version of SAP EWM, embedded within SAP S/4HANA. It provides essential warehouse management features with no additional licensing costs.
  • Key Features:
    • Basic warehouse processes such as picking, packing, and goods receipt/goods issue.
    • Inventory management and stock transfers.
    • Integration with SAP S/4HANA for seamless data flow.
  • Best Suited For: Small to medium-sized businesses or warehouses with simple processes that don’t require advanced features like automation or complex workflows.

4. Advanced EWM (Embedded within SAP S/4HANA)

  • What It Is: Advanced EWM is the more feature-rich version of SAP EWM, offering a comprehensive set of advanced warehouse management functionalities within SAP S/4HANA.
  • Key Features:
    • Complex warehouse operations, including cross-docking, wave management, slotting, and labor management.
    • Full support for automation systems (AGVs, conveyors).
    • Advanced stock management, including batch management and serialization.
    • Yard management, kitting, and value-added services.
  • Best Suited For: Medium to large businesses with complex warehouse processes that require high levels of automation and optimization.

5. Decentralized EWM

  • What It Is: Decentralized EWM is a stand-alone version of SAP EWM that operates on a separate server from the SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA system, offering greater flexibility and scalability.
  • Key Features:
    • Runs independently from the SAP ERP system for improved flexibility.
    • Comprehensive functionality similar to Advanced EWM.
    • Designed for large warehouses with high transaction volumes and complex operations.
    • Multi-warehouse support and real-time process control.
  • Best Suited For: Large, global enterprises with multiple warehouses, complex operations, and high transaction volumes, where independence from the ERP system is crucial for uninterrupted operations.

Key Factors to Consider When Choosing the Right SAP Warehouse Management Solution

1. Warehouse Complexity

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are designed for businesses with straightforward warehouse operations. They are ideal if your processes are mostly manual, and you don’t require complex workflows or advanced automation.
  • Advanced EWM and Decentralized EWM offer more comprehensive features, such as integration with automation, real-time process monitoring, and optimization. These are best for businesses with intricate warehouse setups, multiple locations, or advanced inventory management needs.
  • SAP WM on S/4HANA Public Cloud is suitable for companies seeking a cloud-first approach, providing core functionalities but with scalability and real-time access to inventory data.

2. Automation Needs

  • If your warehouse relies on automation or is planning to integrate automated systems (e.g., conveyors, AGVs, robots), you will need the capabilities of Advanced EWM or Decentralized EWM. These solutions offer robust support for integrating automation with your warehouse processes.
  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are better suited for manual operations or warehouses with low levels of automation.

3. Cloud Adoption Strategy

  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud is the optimal choice if your business is looking to adopt a cloud-based WMS. It provides flexibility, regular updates, and reduced infrastructure costs, making it ideal for companies focused on digital transformation and scalability.
  • For businesses that prefer an on-premise system or already have SAP S/4HANA in place, Embedded EWM (Basic or Advanced) or Decentralized EWM are viable options.

4. Cost and Licensing

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are included with SAP S/4HANA, making them cost-effective solutions for businesses that don’t need the additional features provided by Advanced or Decentralized EWM.
  • Advanced EWM requires additional licensing, but it offers significantly more functionality for businesses with complex warehouse needs.
  • Decentralized EWM comes with higher costs, as it requires separate infrastructure and licensing, but it is essential for large enterprises that need a fully independent warehouse management system.

5. Future Growth and Scalability

  • Stock Room Management and Basic EWM are suitable for businesses with relatively stable warehouse operations that don’t anticipate significant changes in the near future.
  • Advanced EWM and Decentralized EWM offer scalability, making them ideal for growing businesses or enterprises with plans to expand their warehouse operations, implement automation, or add new distribution centers.
  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud provides cloud-native scalability, making it an excellent choice for companies looking to grow their operations quickly and cost-effectively while leveraging cloud infrastructure.

Conclusion: Which SAP Warehouse Management Solution is Right for You?

Choosing the right SAP warehouse management solution depends on your business’s operational complexity, growth plans, and technological direction. Here's a summary of when each solution may be the best fit:

  • Stock Room Management: Best for businesses with simple warehouse operations that are moving from SAP’s classic Warehouse Management (WM) system and want to continue with basic functionality within SAP S/4HANA.
  • SAP Warehouse Management on S/4HANA Public Cloud: Ideal for companies focused on adopting cloud solutions with core warehouse functionality and scalability.
  • Basic EWM: Perfect for small to medium-sized businesses that need essential warehouse management features as part of their SAP S/4HANA system without additional costs.
  • Advanced EWM: Suited for medium to large businesses with more complex warehouse operations, requiring advanced automation, inventory management, and process optimization.
  • Decentralized EWM: The best choice for large, multi-warehouse businesses that need a fully independent WMS, capable of handling high volumes and offering real-time, 24/7 operations.

By assessing your current and future warehouse needs, transaction volumes, levels of automation, and growth strategy, you can make an informed decision and choose the right SAP warehouse management solution to drive operational efficiency and support your business objectives.

entry.title: From CAD to Manufacturing as-a-Service. New Idea-to-Market Public Cloud Scenario available
entry.content:
Overview

The journey started in the mid-1990s with SAP R/3 and the CPI-C based CAD Interfaces, then over to iPPE, CAD Desktop, SAP PLM 7, SAP ECTR and S/4HANA and now to the new SAP PLM – SAP has constantly been supporting customers in their product development and market introduction efforts. Since the first releases we not only focused on typical PLM topics like CAD data management but also always provided an integrated path into the rest of the enterprise like purchasing or manufacturing.

Software for the Public Cloud now allows additional customer groups like growing enterprises, subsidiaries, country organizations or individual departments to benefit, … especially since we recently introduced a missing key capability in the public cloud-based SAP PLM - the new ‘Product Data Integration’ Software-as-a-Service application for public cloud-based CAD Integration. Together with this CAD Integration, we also provided all necessary enhancements to allow a smooth handover of product structures, routings, and products to SAP S/4HANA Cloud supporting downstream processes like purchasing and manufacturing.

What’s the benefit?

For the first time SAP is now offering a completely public cloud-based suite to support all processes relevant from early product definition and development through to manufacturing. As with all public cloud services, the effort that you need for implementing the software and keeping it up to date is reduced dramatically. In addition, you directly receive all the new features and developments – and not just years later. Several extensibility tools continue to allow you to enhance the software, always following the clean core guidance. Altogether, this makes it easier, especially for smaller units, to get started with software support for your product development processes.

Idea-to-Market

To lower the entry barrier for the implementation of SAP PLM, this all should be as simple as possible – especially since this additional deployment option mainly targets smaller units and smaller implementation partners. To provide additional guidance for the implementation, we now deliver process blueprints on SAP’s Business Accelerator Hub. On the Business Accelerator Hub, you can find reference content for all areas in an enterprise: https://api.sap.com/allprocess , documented by us following the same methodology. You’ll find all information for product development under ‘Idea to Market’. The new solution variant ‘Standard Engineering (Discreet Industries) for Cloud Deployment’ is now available here: https://api.sap.com/package/StandardEngineeringDiscreteIndustriesforPublicCloudDeployment/businessprocess. The four categories of diagrams we provide help you to understand with typical enterprise architecture views which business process scope is covered, which components are involved, how is the data flowing through the integrated system landscape, and more:

  • The value flow diagram depicts all business activities supported by the variant
  • The component diagram depicts all solution components needed to implement the business process
  • The data flow diagram depicts key flows of master and transactional data of the variant
  • The process flow diagram, based on the BPMN standard, depicts the process flow and used to specify the collaboration of the different solution components in more detail

From the big picture down to the more detailed process flow diagrams, you as our partners and customers can use this as a template for your implementations.

Several aspects like project management, prototyping, time confirmation will be picked up in future releases. Also engineer-to-order or configure-to-order will be covered in detail later.

What is covered by Idea-to-Market?

The described process starts with an idea for a new product or an improvement resulting from feedback or an ideation campaign. Sometimes you get several similar ideas. Therefore, several ideas can be aggregated into single needs. The ideas and needs are then linked to requirements. With the requirements structuring, a product manager has a clear overview as a basis for a transparent product planning process. From there (mostly for more complex products) either a systems model or a high-level product structure can be directly created. Additionally, CAD engineers can start working on the detailed design and with this also defining the bill-of-material.

Of course, there can be many iterations until the best design is available – these iterations can be managed with SAP PLM’s versioning concept of products and product structures. The built-in visualization service, configuration management, and collaboration features additionally support the development process. Once a certain maturity level has been reached the handover to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can be started. Only then the missing material masters, routings and bills-of-materials are created in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system for further processing.

In the next weeks we will also roll out this reference flow to SAP Cloud ALM and the SAP Signavio Process Navigator.

Summary

SAP offers simplified access to idea-to-market support in the public cloud, especially for smaller or growing companies or units. Availability of detailed process templates helps our customers and partners reduce project risk and accelerate the project implementation. With the new ‘Idea-to-Market: Standard Engineering (Discrete Industries) for Public Cloud Deployment’, you now have a template process description available. Over time, additional content and variants (ETO, CTO or process industries) will be added as well.

Links:

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub: Business Processes: https://api.sap.com/allprocess
  • Idea-to-Market: https://api.sap.com/process/ideaToMarket
  • Documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/I2M?locale=en-US

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.1: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.1 is now available. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is based on the technical product SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

To access the Final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

For further formats and languages of the What's New information, as well as pre-filtered views, please use the following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Help Portal page.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.1 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access the Product Assistance in multiple languages.

For you as a customers, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.1 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

There is a new commenting feature available for What’s New. Authors can now evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. 

To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.
